title: Matching Pairs
intro: `%` jumps between matching brackets — `(` ↔ `)`, `[` ↔ `]`, `{` ↔ `}` — and understands nesting. It is a motion like any other, so `d%` deletes from a bracket through its partner.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the outer `(`. Jump to its matching `)` with `%`.
hints: `%` skips the nested pair automatically — it always finds the true partner.

exercises.instruction: Jump from the opening `{` to the closing `}` of the function with `%`.
Delete the whole argument list: with the cursor on the `(`, press `d%`.
hints: `d%` deletes from the bracket under the cursor through its match, inclusive.

exercises.instruction: It works backward too: from the closing `]`, jump to the opening `[` with `%`.
hints: `%` jumps in whichever direction the partner is.

exercises.instruction: From the first `(`, jump to the very last `)` with a single `%`.
Delete the options object: from the `{`, press `d%` — everything through the `}` goes.
hints: `d%` spans lines just like the motion does.
